prose: Fukui reactivity indices computed for formaldehyde (CH₂O, 4 atoms) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals). Fukui indices were derived from finite-difference Mulliken partial charges across the N, N−1 (cation, doublet), and N+1 (anion, doublet) electronic states. The headline result is the f⁺ value for the most electrophilic atom: C (atom 2), f⁺ = 0.70839. Full per-atom results: O(1): f⁺=0.46814, f⁻=0.46742, f⁰=0.46778, Δf=+0.00071; C(2): f⁺=0.70839, f⁻=0.11244, f⁰=0.41042, Δf=+0.59595; H(3): f⁺=−0.08827, f⁻=0.21008, f⁰=0.06090, Δf=−0.29835; H(4): f⁺=−0.08826, f⁻=0.21006, f⁰=0.06090, Δf=−0.29832. Integrity: status=ok, trustworthy=true. All three integrity checks passed: fukui_charge_conservation_f+ (sum=1.0000), fukui_charge_conservation_f- (sum=1.0000), schema_header. No warnings were reported. The carbonyl carbon is the dominant electrophilic site and the oxygen is the dominant nucleophilic site, consistent with formaldehyde's known reactivity. Output JSON: formaldehyde_fukui_dft.json. CLI: cli.py fukui formaldehyde.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1.
